response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems engineer with 15+ years of experience, but fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, he lists no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — native-level Japanese is a non-negotiable requirement and constitutes an immediate disqualifier. Beyond the language gap, his C/embedded experience is minimal and dated (one role at National Instruments in 2014-2015 writing C/C++ for a desktop IDE), he has no demonstrated knowledge of the required network security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange), and no LAN switch, Layer 2/3 networking, or Linux kernel-level work appears anywhere in his history. His primary career trajectory is web, cloud, and full-stack development, which does not transfer to embedded firmware security.
strengths: Broad multi-language background including C/C++, Linux, and embedded contexts from earlier roles
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) indicate disciplined engineering habits
Demonstrated ability to work across hardware and software layers at Altom Consulting (robotics, DevOps, embedded tooling)
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a stated native-level hard requirement that is an absolute disqualifier for this position
No experience with any of the required security domains: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, device attack/tamper detection, or encryption key exchange
No LAN switch, Layer 2/3 networking, or Linux kernel modification experience; career is overwhelmingly web and cloud-oriented
